entities.name: COTTON
entities.description: The crop species used in the study for genomic DNA isolation and genotyping

entities.name: GENOMIC DNA ISOLATION
entities.description: The process of extracting genomic DNA from plant tissues, which is complicated by secondary metabolites in cotton

entities.name: SECONDARY METABOLITES
entities.description: Chemical compounds present in cotton that are inhibitory to PCR amplification

entities.name: PCR AMPLIFICATION
entities.description: The process of amplifying specific DNA sequences, which can be inhibited by secondary metabolites

entities.name: RADICLE TIPS
entities.description: The tips of the embryonic root in cotton seedlings that yield high-quality DNA amenable for PCR

entities.name: PCR
entities.description: Polymerase Chain Reaction, a method used for genotyping and melting curve analysis

entities.name: ADVENTITIOUS ROOTS
entities.description: Roots that form in cotton seedlings after radicle tip excision, allowing the seedlings to retain viability

entities.name: GENOTYPING
entities.description: The process of determining the genetic constitution of an individual, used to distinguish homozygotes from heterozygotes

entities.name: MELTING CURVE ANALYSIS
entities.description: A PCR-based analysis method used for genomic study in cotton

entities.name: HOMOZYGOTES
entities.description: Individuals having two identical alleles for a particular gene, distinguished through genotyping

entities.name: HETEROZYGOTES
entities.description: Individuals having two different alleles for a particular gene, distinguished through genotyping

entities.name: HYBRID SEED PURITY TESTING
entities.description: A method used to verify the genetic purity of hybrid cotton seeds

relationships.description: is applied to breeding populations of
